skill.name: develop-backend
skill.description: Guides Spring Boot and MyBatis backend implementation, including REST APIs, Flyway database migrations, controller, service, mapper layers, tests, and local verification.
skill.summary: Implement and verify Spring Boot/MyBatis backend features with project conventions.
skill.author: Crearize

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, and entropy are false positives from Markdown fences, Japanese prose, and documentation file names. The skill does contain legitimate external command instructions for Git, search, Gradle checks, and server startup, so it should be published with a command-execution warning.

medium_findings.title: Legitimate External Command Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run local shell commands for branch checks, file listing, source search, Gradle validation, and server startup. These commands match backend development work, but they execute project scripts and can fetch remote Git changes, so users should review commands before running them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in Markdown code blocks and are directly intended for execution during development. The context is legitimate build and verification work, with no evidence of stealth, credential access, or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited areas are Markdown code fences and inline formatting. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution or hidden command substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static locations correspond to Markdown fences or template formatting in SKILL.md. Semantic review found no Ruby source file and no executable backtick expression.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to documentation names and ordinary prose, including Markdown file references. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines reference .md documentation files and project documents, not cryptographic code. The file is a prose skill guide, not an implementation containing crypto primitives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Content
low_findings.description: The entropy alert is consistent with Japanese text and dense Markdown instructions. No evidence found of binary payloads, encrypted blobs, encoded scripts, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file begins with normal YAML front matter and Japanese description text. Review of the file showed readable instructions throughout, not encoded or encrypted data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell snippets for git branch checks, git pull, grep, ls, Gradle checks, and bootRun. This is expected for a backend development skill, but command execution remains a user-visible operational risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and easy to verify in SKILL.md. Their purpose is development verification, but they can execute repository-controlled build logic.

content.user_title: Build Spring Boot Backends with MyBatis
content.value_statement: Backend work often fails when API, database, and test changes drift from project standards. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through implementation, documentation, tests, and local verification.
actual_capabilities: Plans RESTful Spring Boot controller, service, mapper, entity, and DTO changes.
Directs updates to OpenAPI, database design, and error-code documentation.
Guides Flyway migration creation and MyBatis XML mapper implementation.
Requires JUnit and Mockito tests with coverage expectations.
Checks unused code, imports, comments, lint results, build results, and server startup.
Encourages reuse of existing utilities, AOP logging, and exception handlers.
limitations: It is tailored to a specific Spring Boot and MyBatis project structure.
It assumes Gradle, Flyway, MyBatis, JUnit 5, Mockito, and related documents exist.
It does not provide a reusable backend framework or generated source package.
It requires users to review local commands before execution.

use_cases.title: Add a REST API Feature
use_cases.description: Implement a new backend endpoint with controller, service, mapper, DTO, documentation, and tests.

use_cases.title: Ship a Database-Backed Change
use_cases.description: Create Flyway migrations, update database design notes, and align MyBatis mappings with schema changes.

use_cases.title: Verify Project Standards
use_cases.description: Run project checks, inspect IDE warnings, confirm coverage, and report implementation results consistently.

prompt_templates.title: Implement a Simple Endpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for one API with no schema change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to implement a Spring Boot endpoint for [feature]. Follow existing controller, service, mapper, and DTO patterns. Update API documentation and add unit tests.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Database Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task with table or column changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add backend support for [feature] with a Flyway migration. Update database-design.md, MyBatis mappings, service logic, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Backend Logic
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task that must preserve behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to refactor [backend area]. Reuse product.util helpers, avoid duplicate logging, keep ExceptionHandler behavior, and verify with Gradle checks.

prompt_templates.title: Deliver a Complete Backend Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task across API, database, tests, and validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to deliver [feature] end to end. Include REST API design, migration, mapper XML, service transactions, error codes, OpenAPI updates, tests, coverage review, and server startup verification.

output_examples.input: Add an endpoint for user profile retrieval.
output_examples.output: A completed backend change plan with controller, service, mapper, DTO, OpenAPI, tests, validation results, and a completion checklist.

output_examples.input: Add a new table for feature settings.
output_examples.output: A migration-focused implementation summary covering schema design, mapper updates, service behavior, database documentation, and verification steps.

output: Lint, tests, coverage, and build results summarized.
Server startup and API behavior confirmed.

best_practices: Review existing utilities, logging aspects, and exception handlers before adding new code.
Keep API, database, error-code, and feature documentation aligned with implementation changes.
Run local checks and review IDE warnings before reporting completion.
anti_patterns: Do not duplicate automatic AOP request, response, or SQL logging.
Do not add broad try-catch blocks when existing exception handlers should manage errors.
Do not leave unused code, unused imports, or commented-out implementation fragments.

faq.question: What projects does this skill fit best?
faq.answer: It fits Spring Boot projects that use MyBatis, Flyway, Gradle, JUnit 5, Mockito, and layered backend architecture.

faq.question: Can it create database migrations?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides Flyway migration naming, schema updates, MyBatis changes, and database documentation updates.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the agent to run Gradle checks, but users should approve local command execution first.

faq.question: Does it handle API documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes OpenAPI updates and requires operation details to match implemented controller behavior.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium warning. It contains normal development commands, but no malicious intent or prompt injection was found.

faq.question: What should users review before use?
faq.answer: Users should review Git, Gradle, and server startup commands before execution, especially in unfamiliar repositories.
